title: Dual sign-off pass: oncologist accuracy/currency + advocate tone/usefulness on all ship-slated pages
context: Ewing-Family-Guide is a free, plain-language guide for families, caregivers, and AYA patients facing a Ewing sarcoma diagnosis. It ONLY restates and organizes vetted-authority information (NCI/PDQ, COG, ESMO, CCLG), with a citation on every assertion, at a health-literacy reading level. This is HIGH risk-tier, patient-facing health content: nothing patient-facing ships until both a credentialed pediatric/sarcoma oncologist AND a patient/caregiver advocate sign off (version-scoped, either can block). No delivery partner, oncologist reviewer, or patient-advocate reviewer is yet secured. This is the blocking dual-review gate itself: oncologist and advocate sign-off recorded per page and per version on all ship-slated pages. Either reviewer may block; no page publishes without both sign-offs for its current version. Reachable only once both reviewers are secured. Binding cancer guardrails apply: open-access/aggregate/de-identified educational sources only; controlled-access (dbGaP, EGA, individual-level biobanks) and any identifiable patient data are out of scope; verify each source license; provenance on every assertion; no medical advice, diagnosis, or individual prognosis.
objective: Run and record the blocking dual sign-off pass over all ship-slated pages: oncologist (accuracy/currency/non-advice framing/time-critical surfacing) and advocate (tone/dignity/clarity/usefulness), version-scoped, in the reviewers ledger.
acceptanceCriteria: Oncologist sign-off recorded per page for clinical accuracy, currency, non-advice framing, and correct surfacing of time-critical items
Advocate sign-off recorded per page for tone, dignity, clarity, sensitivity, usefulness
Sign-off is version-scoped; either reviewer may block; unresolved disputes escalate per Governance; no page publishes without both sign-offs for its current version
